request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: FDA approves Retatrutide in 2026?
Resolution criteria: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the U.S. Food and Drug Administration formally
approves Retatrutide (LY3437943, Eli Lilly's triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon receptor
agonist) for any indication by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Approval must be
via formal FDA authorization — a New Drug Application (NDA) or Biologics License
Application (BLA) approval letter. Excludes Emergency Use Authorizations (EUA),
compassionate use designations, tentative approvals, and approvable letters.

Retatrutide targets obesity, type 2 diabetes, and related metabolic conditions.
Phase 3 clinical trial data is expected in 2026, with NDA submission expected no
earlier than late 2026, making a 2026 FDA decision unlikely but possible on
accelerated review pathways.

Conditions:
  A: FDA NDA approval by 2026 deadline — FDA issues an NDA approval letter for Retatrutide (LY3437943) for any indication by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Accelerated approval qualifies. Excludes EUA, compassionate use, tentative or approvable letters.
  B: FDA BLA approval by 2026 deadline — FDA issues a BLA approval letter for Retatrutide (LY3437943) for any indication by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Accelerated approval qualifies. Excludes EUA, compassionate use, tentative or approvable letters.
Settlement entities: ['Food and Drug Administration', 'Center for Drug Evaluation and Research', 'Eli Lilly and Company', 'Office of New Drugs']

Record title: Attorney General James Leads Bipartisan Effort to Curb Sales of Illegal Vaping Products
Record date: 2026-04-28
Record regulator: Office of the Attorney General of New York
Record topic: New York State Office of the Attorney General
Record entities: ['American Express Company', 'Attorney General James', 'Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives', 'California Office of the Attorney General', 'City of New York', 'Connecticut Office of the Attorney General', 'Delaware Department of Justice', 'Hawaii Department of the Attorney General', 'Illinois Attorney General Office', 'Indiana Attorney General Office', 'Maine Attorney General Office', 'Maryland Attorney General Office', 'Massachusetts Office of Attorney General', 'Michigan Department of Attorney General', 'Nevada Attorney General Office', 'New Hampshire Attorney General Office', 'New Jersey Attorney General Office', 'New Mexico Department of Justice', 'New York Attorney General Office', 'North Carolina Department of Justice', 'Oregon Attorney General Office', 'Pennsylvania Attorney General Office', 'PayPal', 'Puerto Rico Department of Justice', 'Rhode Island Office of the Attorney General', 'Sezzle', 'State of Wisconsin Department of Justice', 'Stripe', 'Tennessee Office of the Attorney General', 'U.S. Food and Drug Administration', 'Visa', 'Washington State Attorney General’s Office', 'Wisconsin Department of Justice', 'Block', 'Citi Group', 'Capital One']

response.one_line_why: State AG crackdown on illegal vaping products doesn't affect Eli Lilly's Retatrutide NDA/BLA approval prospects at FDA.
